issues: lower empty slot rows have low contrast and create visual clutter
panel occludes most of the game world when open
reasoning: The inventory panel is immediately locatable: the golden pixel-art border frame contrasts sharply against the dark teal dungeon tiles, and the cream/beige interior creates a strong light-on-dark silhouette readable in well under 0.5s. The decorative corner ornaments and scrolled navigation arrows at top/bottom give it a clear, distinctive shape that cannot be confused with game-world entities or floor tiles. The pixel-art border style is consistent with the game HUD, action buttons, and other UI chrome — it reads as native, not pasted. Against the dark areas in the upper-left quadrant, the golden border remains visible. The main weakness is the bottom half of the grid: empty placeholder slots are low-contrast beige-on-beige shapes that blend together, adding visual noise without informational value. Overall the panel is bright, uncluttered in its active-item zone, and fits the Echoes fantasy-pixel aesthetic cleanly.
